tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and glycosuria with low urine specific gravity, suggesting possible renal impairment. Urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal, but no evidence of infection (negative nitrite/leukocytes). Blood glucose and HbA1c are not elevated, making diabetes unlikely. Mild hyperchloremia is present. Recommend further nephrological evaluation for possible chronic kidney disease and assessment for non-diabetic causes of renal dysfunction.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
